audit_reporting_templates.overview: Templates and structures for generating comprehensive, actionable audit reports. These templates ensure consistency and completeness across different audit types.

main_audit_report_template.title: Repository Audit Report Template
main_audit_report_template.structure:
# IBM FileNet Repository Audit Report
**Audit Date:** [Date] **Repository:** [Object Store Name] **Auditor:** Bob (AI Repository Auditor) **Scope:** [Full Audit / Specific Focus Area]
---
## Executive Summary
### Key Findings - **Total Document Classes:** [Number] - **Total Properties:** [Number] ([Custom] custom, [System] system) - **Total Documents:** [Approximate count] - **Critical Issues:** [Number] - **Optimization Opportunities:** [Number]
### Priority Recommendations 1. [High priority recommendation 1] 2. [High priority recommendation 2] 3. [High priority recommendation 3]
### Expected Benefits - [Benefit 1: e.g., Reduced complexity] - [Benefit 2: e.g., Improved performance] - [Benefit 3: e.g., Better governance]
---
## 1. Repository Overview
### 1.1 Class Architecture - **Root Classes Analyzed:** [List] - **Document Classes:** [Number] - **Folder Classes:** [Number] - **Other Classes:** [Number]
### 1.2 Naming Conventions Observed - [Pattern 1: e.g., HR_ prefix for HR documents] - [Pattern 2: e.g., usr1_, usr2_ for user-specific classes] - [Pattern 3: e.g., Demo/Test classes present]
### 1.3 Integration Points - [Integration 1: e.g., SAP integration via SAP* properties] - [Integration 2: e.g., Salesforce integration via SF* properties] - [Integration 3: e.g., DocuFlow integration]
---
## 2. Document Class Analysis
### 2.1 Class Inventory
| Class Name | Display Name | Parent | Properties | Documents | Status | |------------|--------------|--------|------------|-----------|--------| | [ClassName] | [Display] | [Parent] | [Count] | [Count] | [Active/Unused] |
### 2.2 Class Categories
#### Active Core Classes (High Usage) Classes with more than 100 documents, clear business purpose, actively used.
**Classes:** - **[ClassName]** - [Description] - Documents: [Count] - Properties: [Count] - Purpose: [Business purpose] - Recommendation: Keep and optimize
#### Specialized Classes (Moderate Usage) Classes with 10-100 documents, specific business need.
**Classes:** - **[ClassName]** - [Description] - Documents: [Count] - Recommendation: [Keep with documentation / Review]
#### Candidate for Consolidation Classes with overlapping purposes or low usage.
**Classes:** - **[ClassName1]**, **[ClassName2]**, **[ClassName3]** - Issue: [Duplicate functionality / Similar purpose] - Recommendation: Consolidate into [NewClassName]
#### Legacy/Unused Classes Classes with minimal or no documents.
**Classes:** - **[ClassName]** - [Count] documents - Recommendation: Deprecate and remove
### 2.3 Key Findings
#### Finding 1: [Issue Title] **Severity:** High / Medium / Low **Description:** [Detailed description of the issue] **Impact:** [Business and technical impact] **Examples:** - [Specific example 1] - [Specific example 2] **Recommendation:** [Specific action to take]
---
## 3. Property Analysis
### 3.1 Property Statistics
| Class | Total Props | Custom Props | System Props | Searchable | Integration | |-------|-------------|--------------|--------------|------------|-------------| | [Class] | [Count] | [Count] | [Count] | [Count] | [Count] |
### 3.2 Property Categories
#### System Properties Standard FileNet properties present across all classes: - Identity properties (Id, Creator, Owner) - Version control properties - Audit properties (DateCreated, DateLastModified) - Content management properties
#### Business Properties Custom properties specific to business needs: - **[PropertyName]** ([DataType]) - [Description] - Used in: [ClassList] - Searchable: Yes/No - Population rate: [Percentage]
#### Integration Properties Properties supporting external system integration: - **SAP Properties:** [List] - **Salesforce Properties:** [List] - **Other Integration Properties:** [List]
### 3.3 Property Issues
#### Inconsistent Naming Properties with similar purposes but different names: - [Property1], [Property2], [Property3] - Recommendation: Standardize to [StandardName]
#### Unused Properties Properties with low or zero population: - **[PropertyName]** - Population rate: [Percentage] - Recommendation: [Remove / Document usage / Review]
#### Missing Descriptions Properties without clear descriptions: - [PropertyList] - Recommendation: Add descriptions for clarity
---
## 4. Folder Structure Analysis
### 4.1 Folder Organization
**Root Folders:** - [FolderPath1] - [FolderPath2] - [FolderPath3]
**Organization Patterns:** - [Pattern 1: e.g., By department] - [Pattern 2: e.g., By project] - [Pattern 3: e.g., By date]
### 4.2 Folder Hierarchy
**Average Depth:** [Number] levels **Deepest Path:** [Path] ([Number] levels) **Shallowest Path:** [Path] ([Number] levels)
### 4.3 Folder Issues
#### Empty Folders Folders with no documents: - [FolderPath1] - [FolderPath2] - Recommendation: Remove or document purpose
#### Inconsistent Naming Folders with inconsistent naming conventions: - [Example inconsistencies] - Recommendation: Establish naming standards
---
## 5. Document Type Analysis
### 5.1 Document Types by Class
| Class | Document Type Property | Common Values | Distribution | |-------|------------------------|---------------|--------------| | [Class] | [PropertyName] | [Value1, Value2] | [Percentages] |
### 5.2 Classification Accuracy
**Sample Size:** [Number] documents reviewed **Correctly Classified:** [Percentage] **Misclassified:** [Percentage]
**Misclassification Examples:** - [Document] in [Class] should be in [CorrectClass] - Reason: [Explanation]
---
## 6. Integration Assessment
### 6.1 SAP Integration **Classes Using SAP Integration:** [Number] **SAP Properties Found:** - SAPEmployeeID - SAPDocId - [Other properties]
**Status:** Active / Inactive / Partially Used **Recommendation:** [Maintain / Review / Document]
### 6.2 Salesforce Integration **Classes Using Salesforce Integration:** [Number] **Salesforce Properties Found:** - SFLinkTrigger - SfSalesforceRelationships - [Other properties]
**Status:** Active / Inactive / Partially Used **Recommendation:** [Maintain / Review / Document]
### 6.3 Other Integrations [List and describe other integration points]
---
## 7. Recommendations
### 7.1 High Priority (Immediate Action)
#### Recommendation 1: [Title] **Priority:** High **Effort:** Quick Win / Short-term / Long-term **Description:** [Detailed recommendation] **Expected Benefits:** - [Benefit 1] - [Benefit 2] **Action Steps:** 1. [Step 1] 2. [Step 2] 3. [Step 3]
### 7.2 Medium Priority (Short-term)
#### Recommendation 2: [Title] **Priority:** Medium **Effort:** [Effort level] **Description:** [Detailed recommendation] **Expected Benefits:** [Benefits] **Action Steps:** [Steps]
### 7.3 Low Priority (Long-term)
#### Recommendation 3: [Title] **Priority:** Low **Effort:** [Effort level] **Description:** [Detailed recommendation] **Expected Benefits:** [Benefits] **Action Steps:** [Steps]
---
## 8. Implementation Roadmap
### Phase 1: Quick Wins (Weeks 1-2) - [ ] [Action item 1] - [ ] [Action item 2] - [ ] [Action item 3]
### Phase 2: Consolidation (Weeks 3-6) - [ ] [Action item 1] - [ ] [Action item 2] - [ ] [Action item 3]
### Phase 3: Governance (Weeks 7-10) - [ ] [Action item 1] - [ ] [Action item 2] - [ ] [Action item 3]
### Phase 4: Optimization (Ongoing) - [ ] [Action item 1] - [ ] [Action item 2] - [ ] [Action item 3]
---
## 9. Risk Assessment
| Risk | Impact | Probability | Mitigation | |------|--------|-------------|------------| | [Risk 1] | High/Medium/Low | High/Medium/Low | [Mitigation strategy] |
---
## 10. Success Metrics
| Metric | Current | Target | Timeline | |--------|---------|--------|----------| | Total document classes | [Number] | [Target] | [Timeline] | | Classes without documentation | [Number] | 0 | [Timeline] | | Duplicate classes | [Number] | 0 | [Timeline] | | Property population rate | [Percentage] | 80%+ | [Timeline] |
---
## Appendices
### Appendix A: Complete Class List [Link to separate CSV/JSON file or detailed table]
### Appendix B: Property Matrix [Link to separate file with all properties by class]
### Appendix C: Consolidation Candidates [Detailed list of classes to consolidate]
### Appendix D: SQL Queries Used [Document queries used for analysis]
---
## Conclusion
[Summary of audit findings and next steps]
**Contact:** [Your contact information] **Next Review Date:** [Date]

artifact.name: class_inventory_csv
artifact.title: Class Inventory (CSV Format)
artifact.structure: Class Symbolic Name,Display Name,Parent Class,Total Properties,Custom Properties,Document Count,Status,Recommendation HRDocument,HR Document,Document,109,44,150,Active,Keep and optimize Invoice,Invoice,Document,85,20,75,Active,Consolidate with Invoice_ach Invoice_ach,Invoice ACH,Document,82,18,23,Active,Consolidate into Finance_Invoice DemoDocument,Demo Document,Document,65,0,0,Unused,Remove

artifact.name: property_matrix_csv
artifact.title: Property Matrix (CSV Format)
artifact.structure: Property Name,Data Type,Cardinality,Searchable,Class1,Class2,Class3,Description EmployeeID,STRING,SINGLE,Yes,HRDocument,,,Employee identifier FirstName,STRING,SINGLE,Yes,HRDocument,,,Employee first name Department,STRING,SINGLE,Yes,HRDocument,Invoice,Contract,Department assignment

artifact.name: consolidation_plan
artifact.title: Class Consolidation Plan
artifact.structure:
# Class Consolidation Plan
## Consolidation Group 1: Invoice Classes
### Current Classes - Invoice (75 documents) - Invoice_ach (23 documents) - JKJInvoice (12 documents)
### Proposed Consolidated Class **Name:** Finance_Invoice **Properties:** Combined properties from all three classes **New Property:** InvoiceType (STRING, Choice List: Standard, ACH, JKJ)
### Migration Steps 1. Create Finance_Invoice class with combined properties 2. Add InvoiceType property with choice list 3. Migrate documents from Invoice → Finance_Invoice (InvoiceType = Standard) 4. Migrate documents from Invoice_ach → Finance_Invoice (InvoiceType = ACH) 5. Migrate documents from JKJInvoice → Finance_Invoice (InvoiceType = JKJ) 6. Update workflows and integrations 7. Deprecate old classes 8. Remove after 30-day grace period
### Expected Benefits - Reduced class count by 2 - Simplified maintenance - Consistent invoice handling - Easier reporting across invoice types

guideline.rule: Use # for main sections, ## for subsections, ### for sub-subsections
Use **bold** for emphasis on key findings
Use `code formatting` for class names, property names, and technical terms
Use tables for structured data comparison
Use numbered lists for sequential steps
Always include header row with column names
Use alignment for better readability (left for text, right for numbers)
Keep tables concise - move large tables to appendices
Include totals or summaries where appropriate
Provide specific action steps, not vague suggestions
Always use real class names and property names from the audit
Provide specific document counts and statistics
Include concrete examples for each finding

step.action: Create report file at start of audit
step.details: Use filename format: Repository_Audit_Report_YYYY-MM-DD.md Include basic structure with all main sections
Write executive summary after completing all analysis sections to ensure it accurately reflects findings
Add findings to each section as you complete that phase of analysis Don't wait until the end to document everything
Create CSV files for class inventory and property matrix Create separate files for detailed data Link to these files from main report
Check for completeness, clarity, and actionability Ensure all recommendations have specific action steps Verify all statistics and counts are accurate

category.name: completeness
category.item: All requested audit areas are covered
Supporting data is included or referenced

category.name: clarity
category.item: Technical terms are explained or obvious from context
Tables and charts are properly labeled

category.name: accuracy
category.item: All class names and property names are correct
Document counts and statistics are accurate
